description_long: This object is a vintage biplane with a distinctive yellow and white color scheme, featuring black crosses on the wings and tail, reminiscent of early 20th-century military aircraft. The airplane has two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked vertically, supported by struts and wires, and a single wooden propeller mounted at the nose. The fuselage is slender and tapers toward the tail, which includes a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The cockpit is open and positioned just behind the upper wing, likely designed for a single pilot. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, all fixed and non-retractable. The airplane is displayed on a circular, dark brown base, simulating a ground surface.
description: A vintage yellow and white biplane with black crosses, open cockpit, and fixed landing gear, displayed on a circular base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wooden propeller, dual wings, and open cockpit behind the upper wing.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the yellow nose, black crosses on the wings, and the tail section.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail assembly, horizontal and vertical stabilizers, and the full wingspan.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view emphasizing the cockpit position, wing struts, and tail details.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and supported by struts; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing.
